description: Closely spaced tones blur into one another because a rectangular analysis window spread their energy across many bins.
body: Failure mode: a non-periodic block analyzed with an implicit rectangular window produces high side lobes that smear each tone across neighboring bins. Detection signals: broad skirts around every peak, an inability to separate two known nearby tones, and amplitude readings that vary with block alignment. Do not report amplitudes from a rectangular-windowed spectrum when accuracy matters. Safer fallback: apply a tapered window such as Hann or Blackman-Harris and re-measure with the window's known scaling correction.
